title: Repository, Locks, Metadata, and Provenance Scaffold
objective: Create an independent, portable, locked, testable, and evidence-producing project foundation without implementing product workflows.
scope: Version, citation, license, and CodeMeta metadata
PromptEvent, Ticket, HumanDecision, Run, AssetRights, and ReleaseManifest schemas
CI, security scanning, SBOM, tests, and P001 evidence

acceptance.criterion: A documented single command restores the locked Python and R environments from a clean checkout.
Format, lint, type, unit, metadata, and schema checks pass with zero errors.
Two frozen Python syncs preserve the lock and direct dependency versions; the R lock reports a consistent state.
PromptEvent capture modes and hashes validate without representing summary-only content as native.
HumanDecision records distinguish human ownership from AI assistance and validate against the schema.
Repository scans find no secret, user corpus, or local absolute path in versionable files.
Container base references are digest-pinned; local build verification is reported truthfully.
